channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump redesignates Houthis as foreign terrorist organization
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday directed his administration to relist the Houthis in Yemen as a foreign terrorist organization (FTO) in an effort to crack down on the Iranian-backed militant group.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Europe posts record year for clean energy use as Trump pulls US toward fossil fuels
item.description: A record 47% of the European Union’s electricity now comes from solar and other renewables, a report Thursday said, in yet another sign of the growing gap between the bloc’s push for clean energy and the new U.S. administration’s pursuit of more fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Sibi Arasu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexican border states prepare migrant shelters as Trump begins deportation campaign
item.description: Mexico was raising sprawling tents on the U.S. border Wednesday as it braced for President Donald Trump to fulfill his pledge to reverse mass migration.
item.dc:creator: Martín Silva Rey And Félix Márquez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Murdoch's UK tabloids apologize to Prince Harry and admit intruding on the late Princess Diana
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Harry claimed a “monumental” victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology for intruding in his life for years, and agreed to pay substantial damages to settle his privacy invasion lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama protests 'threat' of force as Trump pushed to reclaim canal
item.description: The Panamanian government formally raised concerns with the United Nations over President Trump’s threats to retake the Panama Canal, noting any threat of force would violate rules. In a letter to U.N. Secretary General António Guterres, Panama points to U.N.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: 1,772 Black servicemen who died in WWI were ignored in South Africa. At last they are being honored
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The names are carved on poles of African hardwood that are set upright as if reaching for the sun. No one knows where the men they represent were buried.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghans who fled Taliban rule urge Trump to lift refugee program suspension
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghans who fled after the Taliban seized power appealed Wednesday to U.S. President Donald Trump to exempt them from an order suspending the relocation of refugees to the United States, some saying they risked their lives to support U.S. troops.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Witnesses describe chaos and panic at Turkish ski resort as fire ripped through hotel where 79 died
item.description: KARTALKAYA, Turkey (AP) — As flames tore through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey, friends Esra Karakisa and Halime Cetin watched helplessly as the horror unfolded before them.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mother of missing Marine veteran calls Trump admin a 'breath of fresh air' as she continues 12-year search
item.description: Debra Tice, mother of missing Marine veteran and American journalist Austin Tice, has returned to Syria to search for her son who was taken captive in August 2012.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Task Force created in Africa to counter terror fears from Iran and jihadi groups
item.description: South Africa’s Chief Rabbi Dr. Warren Goldstein has created an Anti-Terror Task Force to combat terror from Iran and Jihadi groups globally. The group will also try to protect Christians under attack in Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel says it killed a militant in Gaza as fragile ceasefire holds
item.description: The Israeli military says its soldiers killed an alleged militant in Gaza whom it said posed a threat to its forces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Davos 2025: Trade, tariffs, AI and UN chief Guterres dominate World Economic Forum agenda
item.description: A speech by U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres criticizing fossil fuel use and warning of the perils of artificial intelligence has headlined the agenda at the World Economic Forum’s annual event in Davos.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: As Davos 2025 reacts to Trump tack, EU chief leads defense against climate change
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — As the World Economic Forum’s annual gabfest in the Swiss Alps got into full swing on Tuesday, U.S. President Donald Trump gave everybody something to talk about with his actions on his first day back in office.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia sounds off on Trump’s threat to retake the Panama Canal
item.description: Russia’s foreign ministry has called on Trump to reaffirm the current international agreement surrounding the Panama Canal and to leave it in control of the nation of Panama.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio's first order of business: Meeting Quad's foreign secretaries
item.description: Newly sworn-in Secretary of State Marco Rubio is hitting the ground running Tuesday with meetings with his counterparts in the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue, known as the Quad, which includes India, Australia and Japan, a bloc formed to counter Chinese clout in the Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: Mexico defends sovereignty as US seeks to label cartels as terrorists
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Tuesday that President Donald Trump's executive order moving toward designating drug cartels as foreign terrorist organizations would only impact Mexico if there's close coordination between the two governments.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World leaders react as Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Leaders across the globe reacted to Donald Trump once again entering the White House, with some nations issuing messages of congratulations while others took issue with his inaugural comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump mistakes Spain for a member of the BRICS bloc and repeats the threat of massive tariffs
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump has apparently confused Spain for a member of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, causing some head-scratching and jitters over possible tariffs in Madrid.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military launches large-scale counterterrorism operation in West Bank city
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces announced an operation in the West Bank city of Jenin, which is a stronghold for terror organizations backed by Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Same-sex marriages will soon become legal in Thailand after historic law
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — They have been in a committed relationship for more than 13 years, and even had a wedding in 2019. Since then, Danaya Phonphayung and Sunma Piamboon, both women, have considered themselves a married couple, even if same-sex marriages were not legally recognized.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Landslides and flash floods on Indonesia’s Java island leave 17 dead and 8 missing
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian rescuers recovered the bodies of at least 17 people who were swept away in flash floods or buried under tons of mud and rocks that hit hilly villages on the country’s main island of Java, officials said Tuesday. Eight people were missing.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Strong earthquake in Taiwan injures 27 and causes scattered damage
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A 6.4-magnitude earthquake struck southern Taiwan early Tuesday, leaving 27 people with minor injuries and some reported damage. The quake hit at 12:17 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wake up and spend more on defense, Macron tells Europe as Trump takes office
item.description: CESSON-SEVIGNE, France (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron called on Europe Monday to “wake up” and spend more on defense in order to reduce its reliance on the United States for its security, in a speech to the French military as Donald Trump returns to power.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's population falls for a third straight year, posing challenges for its government and economy
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's population fell last year for the third straight year, its government said Friday, pointing to further demographic challenges for the world's second most populous nation, which is now facing both an aging population and an emerging shortage of working age people.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli president calls Trump a 'true friend' on Inauguration Day, praises his work to release hostages
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog is praising Donald Trump as a "true friend" of Israel, thanking him for his "commitment to bringing all our hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freed Israeli hostage speaks out for first time, says she has 'returned to my life' following Hamas captivity
item.description: Emily Damari, one of the first hostages freed by Hamas in the cease-fire agreement, is speaking out for the first time following her release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners as part of cease-fire deal to free hostages
item.description: Israel released 90 Palestinian prisoners to cheering crowds in the West Bank after Hamas returned three hostages that were being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bittersweet rejoicing as first hostages return to Israel after 471 days in captivity
item.description: Three hostages return to Israel after 471 days in Hamas captivity under a cease-fire deal. Israelis celebrate their return but face concerns about the release of convicted Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German ambassador warns Trump will 'undermine' democratic principles with 'maximum disruption' agenda: report
item.description: In a confidential briefing document, Germany's ambassador to the United States Andreas Michaelis warned that Donald Trump will "undermine" democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to expect as Israel-Hamas cease-fire goes into effect on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli government approved the deal early Saturday morning for a cease-fire in Gaza and the release of hostages captured after Hamas' terrorist attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Cabinet approves a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet approved a deal early Saturday for a ceasefire in Gaza that would release dozens of hostages held there and pause the 15-month war with Hamas, bringing the sides a step closer to ending their deadliest and most destructive fighting ever.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says 'last-minute crisis' with Hamas holding up approval of Gaza truce and hostage deal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said Thursday his Cabinet won’t meet to approve the agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages until Hamas backs down, accusing the group of reneging on parts of the agreement in an atte
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
